ideaBrief.title: Risk Manager
ideaBrief.audience: University students in computer science and project management courses
ideaBrief.coreMetaphor: Managing a software project as a high-stakes adventure filled with risks and challenges
ideaBrief.successVision: Players develop practical risk management skills through immersive gameplay while enjoying a retro-themed simulation

state.problemStatement: Students lack hands-on experience in identifying, assessing, and mitigating project risks in software development
state.targetPopulation: Undergraduate and graduate students in computer science, software engineering, and project management programs
successCriteria: Achieve positive synergy bonuses through team composition
bloomOutcomes: Apply risk assessment techniques in project scenarios
Analyze the impact of team morale on project success
Evaluate different strategies for risk mitigation
Create contingency plans for project challenges
state.narrativeTheme: Futuristic software development in 2026 where project managers navigate technical risks, team dynamics, and deadline pressures
state.gameOverview: Risk Manager is a 30-day simulation game where players manage a software development project called PROJECT: NEON. Players must hire and manage a team, handle daily risks and events, make strategic decisions through dilemmas, and deliver the project on time while maintaining budget and morale.
state.sdtAutonomy: Players have freedom to choose team composition, risk mitigation strategies, and daily management decisions
state.sdtCompetence: Success in risk mitigation and project delivery builds player competence through skill-based challenges
state.sdtRelatedness: Team synergy mechanics and morale systems create social connections between virtual team members

rewardStructure.milestone_completion: Points and money rewards for completing project milestones
rewardStructure.risk_mitigation: Bonus money and morale boosts for successful risk handling
rewardStructure.synergy_bonuses: Extra productivity from complementary team skills
rewardStructure.critical_successes: Random chance events that provide significant advantages

mechanics: Risk Cards: Daily random risk events that must be addressed
Employee Management: Hire, fire, and manage team members with different skills
Daily Progress System: Advance project through daily actions
Morale System: Maintain team motivation through various means
Budget Management: Allocate money for hires, upgrades, and risk mitigation
Dilemma Choices: Make tough decisions that affect project outcomes
Critical Success Events: Random positive events that boost progress
Bug Outbreaks: Special risk events that require immediate attention
Upgrade System: Purchase permanent improvements to team capabilities
Contingency Reserve: Emergency fund for critical risk payments
conceptMappings: Risk Identification -> Risk Cards and Events
Risk Assessment -> Cost and Impact Evaluation
Risk Mitigation -> Strategic Choices and Upgrades
Team Dynamics -> Employee Synergy and Morale
Project Planning -> Milestone Tracking and Deadline Management
Budget Control -> Money and Contingency Reserve Management
Decision Making -> Dilemma Choices and Daily Actions
scenarios: Bug Outbreak: Multiple bugs appear simultaneously, requiring immediate fixes
Deadline Crunch: Final days with multiple risks and low morale
Team Conflict: Low morale leads to productivity drops
Budget Crisis: Insufficient funds for necessary risk mitigation
Synergy Opportunity: Hiring complementary skills creates bonuses
Critical Success Chain: Multiple positive events in sequence
gamePhases: Planning Phase: Initial team hiring and upgrade purchases
Early Development: Building momentum with risk management
Mid-Game Challenges: Complex dilemmas and risk combinations
Endgame Pressure: Final push with deadline approaching
Post-Mortem: Review of decisions and outcomes
userStories: As a project manager, I want to hire employees with complementary skills so that I can achieve synergy bonuses
As a player, I want to see daily risk events so that I can make informed mitigation decisions
As a student, I want clear feedback on my decisions so that I can learn from mistakes
As a player, I want to manage my budget carefully so that I can afford critical risk payments
As a learner, I want to experience realistic project challenges so that I can apply concepts in real work
frs: Display daily HUD with money, morale, day counter, and deadline
Generate random risk events each day with mitigation options
Allow hiring/firing of employees with different skill sets
Track project progress through milestone completion
Present dilemmas requiring player choice with consequences
Manage team morale through various game mechanics
Provide upgrade system for permanent improvements
Implement critical success and bug outbreak events
Show post-mortem report with detailed statistics
Support theme customization for visual preferences
nfrs: Game should run smoothly in web browser
Response time for actions should be under 100ms
Visual style should maintain retro pixel aesthetic
Game state should persist during session
Educational content should be accurate and relevant
kpis: Budget efficiency (money spent vs milestones achieved)
state.logPrivacy: All gameplay data is anonymized and aggregated for educational research purposes only
state.dataCollectionPlan: Game logs player decisions, risk outcomes, and completion metrics for analysis of learning effectiveness
supportTeam: Lead Facilitator: Guides gameplay and debrief
Technical Support: Assists with game technical issues
Educational Coordinator: Ensures learning objectives are met
state.facilitatorRole: Introduce the game mechanics, observe student decision-making, facilitate post-game discussion on risk management strategies, and connect gameplay to real-world project management concepts
state.wf_dash: Project dashboard showing progress bars, milestone status, team composition, and current risks
state.wf_asset: Employee skills and upgrade inventory management interface
state.wf_portfolio: Overall project portfolio view with completed milestones and remaining tasks
state.inScope: Core single-player simulation mechanics, risk management system, team management, progress tracking, and educational debrief features
state.outScope: Multiplayer features, advanced AI opponents, mobile app versions, integration with learning management systems, and real-time collaboration tools
state.whyMotivation: Traditional lectures on risk management lack engagement and practical application. This game provides experiential learning through simulated project challenges.
state.howEthics: Students provide informed consent for gameplay data collection, data is anonymized, and findings are used only for educational research and improvement of the learning experience.
